key_insight: A coupled thermo-mechanical ABAQUS model shows that welding-induced temperature gradients drive concentrated residual stress and deformation in 6082 aluminum alloy butt joints.
engineering_relevance: The results help guide welding process optimization and structural design to reduce distortion and improve service performance of aluminum alloy welded structures.
method: Built a 3D temperature-displacement coupled model of a 6082 aluminum alloy butt weld in ABAQUS
Performed transient thermal analysis to compute the weld temperature field
Used the temperature field in mechanical analysis to predict residual stress and deformation
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the weld zone reaches the highest temperature, with residual stress concentrated in the weld seam and heat-affected zone and noticeable deformation near the weld.
watch_item: The study is simulation-based, so accuracy depends on model assumptions, boundary conditions, and material parameters rather than direct experimental validation in the abstract.
paper_title: Thermal-Mechanical Analysis of Residual Stress and Deformation in 6082 Aluminum Alloy Welded Joints
